title: ononis repens subsp. arvensis
intro: Ononis repens subsp. arvensis, also known as field sainfoin or creeping restharrow, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the legume family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic, antispasmodic, and diuretic properties, which can help alleviate conditions such as urinary tract infections and inflammation. The health benefits of Ononis repens subsp. arvensis include its ability to reduce fever, soothe digestive issues, and act as a natural diuretic to promote urination and flush out toxins. Herbal preparations of Ononis repens subsp. arvensis can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
benefits: Ononis repens subsp. arvensis has many benefits, such as being used to treat various health issues, including inflammation, digestive problems, and respiratory issues like bronchitis and asthma. Its antioxidant properties help to reduce oxidative stress, improve cardiovascular health, and even protect against certain types of cancer. Additionally, this plant has been known to improve cognitive function, reduce anxiety and depression, and even support bone health, making it an excellent addition to a healthy lifestyle. By incorporating Ononis repens subsp. arvensis into one's daily routine, individuals can experience improved overall well-being, increased energy levels, and a more positive outlook on life.
actions: Ononis repens subsp. arvensis has many therapeutic actions, such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, antioxidant, and anti-diabetic properties, which help alleviate symptoms of various conditions, including arthritis, wounds, and diabetes. The plant's anti-inflammatory properties can reduce pain and swelling, making it easier for people to engage in daily activities, like gardening or playing with their children. Its antimicrobial properties can prevent infections and promote wound healing, allowing individuals to recover from injuries and illnesses more quickly, and get back to their daily routines. Furthermore, the plant's antioxidant properties can help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress, which can improve overall health and well-being, enabling people to live more active and fulfilling lives.
intro_study: According to "Turkish archives of otorhinolaryngology", Ononis repens subsp. arvensis benefits include reducing nasal congestion and facial pain in patients with acute postviral rhinosinusitis, as part of the herbal preparation Sinulan forte, compared to mometasone furoate nasal spray.
constituents: Ononis repens subsp. arvensis has many active constituents, such as alkaloids, flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. These compounds have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, helping to alleviate symptoms of conditions like arthritis, improve cardiovascular health, and prevent the growth of harmful bacteria. The flavonoids present in Ononis repens subsp. arvensis, such as quercetin and kaempferol, have been found to have anti-cancer properties, while the saponins have been used to treat digestive issues and lower cholesterol levels. By incorporating Ononis repens subsp. arvensis into their daily lives, people can potentially benefit from improved joint health, enhanced immune function, and a reduced risk of chronic diseases like heart disease and certain cancers.
